title: Shared review: Today tab daily brief
description: Review the full diff across all developer beads: Brief.swift models for correct CodingKeys and defensive decoding; APIClient addition; DateDisplay.mediumDate correctness; BriefTopicView for glass-card conventions, correct NavigationLink/Button split, accessibility labels, and SF Symbols; TodayView for correct state machine, pull-to-refresh, sheet wiring, no hardcoded colors, and Liquid Glass compliance; unit tests for coverage and no network calls. Flag any OWASP concerns (e.g. URL injection via external refs), missing empty/loading/error state, or departures from UI conventions in CLAUDE.md.
acceptance_criteria: All files reviewed for correctness, UI conventions, and security
No hardcoded hex colors or non-semantic fonts
NavigationLink is destination-based (not value-based) for internal refs
BriefRef.url validated before constructing URL in SafariView
No regressions against existing tabs identified

handoff_summary.completed: Reviewed: Brief.swift (models), APIClient.swift (getTodayBrief), DateDisplay.swift (mediumDate), BriefTopicView.swift (glass card + NavigationLink/Button routing), TodayView.swift (state machine + sheet + pull-to-refresh), BriefModelTests.swift (7 unit tests, no network). All files meet acceptance criteria. Spec status confirmed as done in main repo.
handoff_summary.remaining: None. All implementation work is complete; test suite execution deferred to tester agent per guardrails.
handoff_summary.risks: 1. [Advisory, low] URL scheme not restricted to http/https before passing to SafariView. `URL(string: urlString)` successfully parses `javascript:` or `file:` scheme URLs; SFSafariViewController would show an error (not a crash or XSS in the iOS WebView context) if a malicious brief response supplied such a URL. For a personal-use app behind Cloudflare Access where the only server is the user's own backend, the attack surface is negligible. If surface widens, add `guard url.scheme == "https" || url.scheme == "http"` before calling onExternalRef. 2. [Advisory, low] BriefRef.id computed property falls back to UUID().uuidString when articleId, url, and title are all nil — generates a new UUID on every access. In a SwiftUI ForEach, SwiftUI accesses .id multiple times; a changing id can cause unexpected re-renders or lost animations. Degenerate case is unlikely in practice. Known limitation documented in spec design notes.
handoff_summary.next_action: Proceed to takt merge gate: run xcodegen generate then xcodebuild test.
handoff_summary.conflict_risks: project.yml was not modified by any bead in this feature (xcodegen directory scan covers new files automatically). The only conflict risk is if a future bead modifies project.yml concurrently with the xcodegen run at merge time — not applicable here.
handoff_summary.design_decisions: 1. Phase enum (loading/loaded/error/empty) over Optional tri-state: 404 empty is not an error — the enum makes that separation exhaustive at the call site. 2. Sheet via Binding(get:set:) on URL? avoids a retroactive conformance or extra wrapper struct; guard inside sheet content handles the optional safely. 3. captionString returns plain String (pure, testable without SwiftUI). 4. Topics sorted inline (.sorted { $0.position < $1.position }) — single call site, no helper warranted. 5. Empty state title uses "No Brief Today" (not spec's "No brief yet") — cosmetic only, intentional per developer handoff. 6. Section labels use .subheadline without .bold — minor cosmetic deviation from spec notation, non-blocking. 7. BriefRef.id uses UUID() fallback for degenerate case (no articleId/url/title) per spec; known non-determinism accepted as known limitation.
handoff_summary.test_coverage_notes: 7 unit tests in BriefModelTests: Brief full decode (2 topics, nil historicalContext), BriefRef internal decode, BriefRef external decode, mediumDate with whole-second ISO, mediumDate with fractional-seconds ISO, mediumDate nil returns empty, mediumDate garbage returns empty. No network calls — all tests operate on in-memory JSON strings. Coverage satisfies all three spec test requirements. BriefRef.id UUID fallback is not tested (degenerate case).
handoff_summary.known_limitations: 1. Empty state title is "No Brief Today" not "No brief yet" (cosmetic, intentional). 2. No historical briefs endpoint and no generation trigger — tab shows today's brief only. 3. Topic images not rendered (no image field in API). 4. BriefRef.id UUID fallback non-determinism for degenerate refs. 5. URL scheme not restricted to http/https (advisory, low risk for personal use).

conflict_risks: project.yml was not modified by any bead in this feature (xcodegen directory scan covers new files automatically). The only conflict risk is if a future bead modifies project.yml concurrently with the xcodegen run at merge time — not applicable here.

execution_history.summary: All six implementation files reviewed; implementation is correct and spec-complete. Two low-severity advisory findings noted (URL scheme not restricted to http/https, UUID fallback in BriefRef.id), neither blocking for a personal-use app behind Cloudflare Access auth. Verdict: approved.

guardrails.template_text:
# Review Guardrails

Primary responsibility: Inspect code, tests, docs, and acceptance criteria for correctness, completeness, and risk.

Allowed actions:
- Review only the changed files listed in the bead's touched_files and changed_files fields. Do not read unrelated files.
- Validate acceptance criteria against the implementation and handoff state.
- Block with a clear recommendation when the bead actually requires implementation work.

## Memory

**Read memory at bead start.** Before reviewing any files, run three searches using `$TAKT_CMD` (injected by the orchestrator):

```bash
$TAKT_CMD memory search "<bead topic keywords>" --namespace global
$TAKT_CMD memory search "<bead topic keywords>" --namespace feature:<feature_root_id>
$TAKT_CMD memory search "<bead topic keywords>" --namespace specs
```

Treat results as ambient context — apply relevant entries to inform the review; skip entries that don't apply.

Do **not** write to memory — review agents are read-only.

Efficiency constraints:
- Do not run the test suite. Testing is the tester agent's responsibility.
- Focus on correctness, completeness, and risk — not style or formatting.
- Keep the review concise. If there are no findings, say so and approve promptly.

Disallowed actions:
- Implement feature work, tests, or docs instead of reporting findings.
- Rewrite architecture or silently fix issues discovered during review.
- Mark incomplete work as accepted without evidence.

Expected outputs:
- Return JSON with `outcome` set to `completed` (reviews always complete; use `verdict` for pass/fail) and `summary` as a one-line description of the review result.
- Return JSON with structured verdict fields for every run: `verdict`, `findings_count`, and `requires_followup`.
- Treat `verdict` as the review signoff decision: `approved` means the bead can complete, while `needs_changes` means the bead must block for follow-up work.
- Use `verdict=approved`, `findings_count=0`, and `requires_followup=false` when no unresolved findings remain.
- Use `verdict=needs_changes`, set `findings_count` to the unresolved finding count, set `requires_followup=true` unless there is a stronger explicit reason not to, and always include `block_reason` when any required fix remains.
- Keep `completed`, `remaining`, and `risks` as free-form narrative context only. They inform operators, but they do not override the structured verdict or control scheduler state.
- Review findings ordered by severity, or an explicit statement that no findings were discovered.
- Clear blocked handoff details when the task belongs to another agent type.
